forecasts.reasoning: Han Duck-soo is a 75-year-old career technocrat who has served under both progressive and conservative administrations, showing little appetite for partisan politics throughout his long career. As Acting President (likely following President Yoon's impeachment or resignation), he faces strong institutional and cultural pressure to remain neutral and manage the upcoming 'June election' fairly, rather than resigning to run for office himself. In similar past crises in South Korea’s democratic history (e.g., Goh Kun in 2004 and Hwang Kyo-ahn in 2017), the acting presidents chose not to run, prioritizing stability over political ambition. Although the People Power Party (PPP) might be desperate to draft him due to a lack of viable candidates, the structural disincentives—combined with his age and lack of an independent political base—make it highly unlikely he will announce a presidential candidacy before May 5, 2025.
